title: Light-band regression protection phase
summary: Lane 3 should spend one cooldown wave protecting the thaw-skirt and back-dune proof bands from readability or recovery regressions while the new support-readable cue lands elsewhere.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the light-band readability regression handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The recent support proofs are good enough that the next lane-3 spend should be protection, not another destination.

execution_order.goal: Add one tiny regression-protection pass for the proof bands.
execution_order.why_now: The route-feel cue should land on top of stable, recoverable spaces.

execution_order.goal: Review the proof bands for density or geometry creep.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3 should stay disciplined while the product focus is on feel, not map growth.

guardrails: No new landmarks, pocket families, or major geometry.
Prioritize readability, recoverability, and proof coverage.
Treat this as a cooldown packet, not a content-growth packet.

main_296_focus.priority_shape: runtime-only regression protection around the two live support-biased proof shelves
preferred_assertions: same-band player position stays inside the local shelf window before inspect
preferred support target is visible alongside at least one nearby non-fit alternative
non-hand-lens comparison remains in the same local band
avoid: biome geometry edits unless a proof fails first
